steps.title: Determine whether the model grows or decays
steps.content:
In $y = a(b)^x$ with $a > 0$:
• if $b > 1$, $y$ increases as $x$ increases (growth);
• if $0 < b < 1$, $y$ decreases as $x$ increases (decay).

The scatterplot shows $y$ decreasing as $x$ increases, so the model represents decay and $b$ must lie strictly between 0 and 1.

steps.title: Estimate $b$ from two points on the fitted curve
steps.content:
Using two readable points such as $(1, 110)$ and $(9, 25)$:
$$\frac{y_2}{y_1} = b^{x_2 - x_1} \;\Rightarrow\; \frac{25}{110} = b^{8}$$
$$b^{8} \approx 0.227 \;\Rightarrow\; b \approx (0.227)^{1/8} \approx 0.83$$

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• A. $0.83$: satisfies $0 < b < 1$ and matches the computed estimate.
  • B. $1.83$: greater than 1, which would mean growth; eliminated.
  • C. $18.36$: greater than 1; eliminated.
  • D. $126.35$: greater than 1; eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
